judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are nearly identical in substance and both hit a, b, and c equally well; Report 2 additionally and explicitly recommends adding a global/env-level kill switch ANDed with the per-tenant flag (expectation d), which Report 1 only gestures at without recommending, making Report 2 the more complete answer.

report_1.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... The specific decision is line 11: `if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None: return True`.

report_2.evidence: Reminders will be sent automatically. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`... Line 11-12 is the deciding logic: if the key is missing from `tenant.settings`... it returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: I would change the return value at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12` from `return True` to `return False` (mirroring the existing pattern but inverting the default), so only clinics that have explicitly flipped the toggle on start sending reminders.

report_2.evidence: I would change the default at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inverting it so a missing/None value returns `False` (i.e., delete the `is None -> True` special case entirely, or change it to return `False`), so reminders only go out for clinics whose admin has explicitly turned the toggle on.

report_1.evidence: Per `git show 25509a7`, `compact_calendar_view_enabled` was introduced first (SLOT-190), with the "default True when key is missing" pattern... Per `git show aa4cf8d`, `auto_send_reminders_enabled` (SLOT-214) was added by copy-pasting that exact three-line pattern into `feature_flags.py:10-13` right below the calendar-view function... the default was inherited by structural copy-paste from an unrelated toggle with a very different risk profile (visual preference vs. an outbound patient-communication feature)

report_2.evidence: `git show aa4cf8d -- backend/app/feature_flags.py` shows `auto_send_reminders_enabled` was added by literally duplicating the structure of `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, which was introduced one commit earlier (`25509a7`, SLOT-190) for a calendar display preference — a pure UI/authoring convenience with essentially zero blast radius if wrongly defaulted on.

Add an env-level master switch that is ANDed with the per-tenant flag before the job actually sends (per project documentation's flag recipe, step 3), so there's a single global kill switch to stop all outbound reminder sends without having to touch every tenant's settings — I found no such switch anywhere in the repo (`infra/main.tf` only defines an `environment` variable).
